hints.text: Coverage mainly **Gen3 trekker** — extremely limited, only Punjab around major temples & tourist sites
**Urdu** (Arabic-related but softer, curvier fonts) + fairly common **English**
hints.uniq_note: Urdu can look almost indistinguishable from Arabic — the softer/curvier nastaliq font style plus English co-signage points to Pakistan rather than the Middle East.

hints.text: Plates **short**, per-region designs — coverage is all Punjab: **green strip left** or fully green plates

shared_with.diff: India uses **long** plates; Pakistan short. Baggy bright trousers → Pakistan, tighter → India.

hints.text: Much coverage in **Mughal gardens** — ordered gardens, stone walls, old temples/fortresses
Brightly coloured **baggy pants** everywhere (Indian trekkers: tighter pants)
Coverage spots only in **Punjab**; several are **hidden coverage** (Map-Making App only)

bullets: Grey car w/ visible right side around **Lahore Fort** (hidden coverage)
Urban trekker in busy narrow streets just south of the fort (hidden)
**Lahore Fort** + iconic Alamgiri Gate; inside: road between tall **red-brick walls**
**Patiala Block** (King Edward Medical Univ.): white colonial building, domes w/ black lines
**Jallo** (east suburb): semi-urban, poor-looking, very thick haze
**Bahria Grand Hotel** (SW suburb): minigolf course + cow statue at the entrance
**Harappa** (W of Sahiwal): trekker from Harappa Museum on a very narrow concrete path
**Hiran Minar** (NW of Sheikhupura): small temple in a lake, single bridge
**Hazrat Baba Bulleh Shah shrine** (W-Kasur): mosque w/ large green dome + 2 green minaret domes
**Pākpattan** (hidden): market street w/ rug roof, busy urban street, narrow alley
**Rohtas Fort** (W of Jhelum): near-black fortress walls
**Patriata** (NE of Islamabad): hilly pine forests, partly from an **aerial lift**
**Changa Manga Forest Park**: NE path angle + narrow channel along the path
